quick_facts.label: Award and specialization
quick_facts.value: ICAR’s minimum standards recognise the postgraduate award as “M.Sc. (Agriculture)” with a disciplinary suffix, rather than a single undifferentiated programme. Listed suffixes include Agronomy, Agricultural Economics, Agricultural Extension & Communication, Entomology, Genetics and Plant Breeding, Soil Science and Agricultural Chemistry, Plant Pathology, Seed Science and Technology, Plant Biotechnology, and others.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry qualification
quick_facts.value: For M.Sc. (Agriculture), ICAR’s published minimum educational requirement is a B.Sc. (Agriculture). Individual universities and entrance bulletins can add subject-specific, marks, domicile, or document requirements.

quick_facts.label: Standard duration
quick_facts.value: ICAR’s common academic regulations specify a minimum residential requirement of two academic years (four semesters) for a master’s degree; the stated maximum is five academic years (ten semesters).

quick_facts.label: National admission route
quick_facts.value: NTA administers ICAR AIEEA (PG) for admission to master’s programmes in agriculture and allied sciences and for ICAR-PG Scholarships. University-specific admissions may use additional or different routes.

quick_facts.label: Fee context
quick_facts.value: Fees vary materially by university and specialization. As one dated institutional example, Punjab Agricultural University lists ₹63,375 for the first semester of its M.Sc. Agricultural Extension Education programme; this should not be treated as a national fee benchmark.

sections.heading: Eligibility after B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture
paragraphs: The direct academic pathway from the parent node is well aligned: ICAR’s published minimum standard for M.Sc. (Agriculture) admission is a B.Sc. (Agriculture). A B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture graduate should nevertheless check the chosen university’s current prospectus, because admission rules can specify an eligible undergraduate subject combination, minimum marks, category provisions, and required certificates.
bullets: The intended M.Sc. discipline matters: applicants should match their undergraduate preparation to the selected specialization and the current university/entrance bulletin.
Final eligibility is determined by the admitting university and the applicable entrance authority for that admission cycle.

sections.heading: Duration and academic structure
paragraphs: Under ICAR’s common academic regulations for postgraduate programmes, the master’s residential requirement is normally two academic years across four semesters. The same regulations set a maximum completion window of five academic years (ten semesters), subject to the institution’s rules. They also contemplate course-work evaluation and submission of a master’s thesis during the prescribed residency period.
bullets: Published maximum duration under the common regulations: 5 academic years / 10 semesters.
The programme combines taught course work, assessment, and supervised research/thesis work.

sections.heading: What the degree covers
paragraphs: M.Sc. (Agriculture) is normally a specialization-led research degree. ICAR’s approved nomenclature spans crop-production and resource fields such as Agronomy, Soil Science and Agricultural Chemistry, and Agro-meteorology and Environmental Sciences; crop-improvement and protection fields such as Genetics and Plant Breeding, Entomology, Plant Pathology, Plant Nematology, Plant Virology, and Seed Science and Technology; and social, technology, and enterprise-facing fields such as Agricultural Economics, Agricultural Extension & Communication, Food Science and Technology, and Agricultural Engineering.

The detailed syllabus depends on the discipline and university. For example, ICAR’s published Agronomy curriculum includes modern crop production, soil fertility and nutrient management, weed and water management, conservation agriculture, organic production systems, research methodology, and biostatistics. A university’s programme may further prescribe core courses, electives/minors, seminars, practical work, and a thesis.
bullets: Choose the specialization first; the same M.Sc. (Agriculture) title can represent substantially different coursework and research.
Expect advanced discipline coursework plus research methods/statistics and a dissertation or thesis component.
Compare the department-level syllabus, facilities, field/laboratory access, and supervisor research areas before applying.

sections.heading: Admission routes and 2026-cycle context
paragraphs: A major national route is ICAR AIEEA (PG), conducted by the National Testing Agency for admission to master’s programmes in agriculture and allied sciences and for the award of ICAR-PG Scholarships. For the 2026–27 cycle, ICAR’s May 11, 2026 notice stated that registration had opened on May 8, 2026 and was scheduled to close on June 7, 2026; these dates are historical context, not future deadlines. Candidates should use the active NTA/ICAR notices and their target university’s prospectus for current schedules, seats, qualifying requirements, counselling procedures, and scholarship terms.

Some institutions also run discipline-specific admissions. For example, PAU’s 2026 notice for M.Sc. (Agri.) Molecular Biology and Biotechnology states that admission is based on GAT-B score plus fulfilment of the university’s prospectus requirements. This illustrates why a candidate should not assume that every M.Sc. (Agriculture) specialization uses exactly the same entrance route.
bullets: Check whether the selected programme admits through ICAR AIEEA (PG), another national test, or its own institutional process.
Read the current information bulletin and the university prospectus before applying; they control eligibility, documents, reservation, fees, counselling, and dates.
Treat any published deadline, seat matrix, or test requirement as cycle-specific.

sections.heading: Costs and funding
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide tuition figure for M.Sc. (Agriculture): charges depend on the university, specialization, category, hostel and mess choices, and yearly revisions. As a dated example available on PAU’s website, the first-semester fee listed for M.Sc. Agricultural Extension Education is ₹63,375. Applicants should obtain the current programme fee table directly from the admitting institution before budgeting.

For eligible entrants through the ICAR route, NTA identifies AIEEA (PG) as the examination used both for master’s admission and for ICAR-PG Scholarships. The applicable bulletin governs scholarship eligibility, award conditions, number of awards, and any updates for a given academic year.
bullets: Budget separately for tuition/academic charges, hostel and mess, field or laboratory charges, deposits, travel, and thesis-related expenses where applicable.
Use an institution’s current prospectus or fee notification rather than third-party fee estimates.
Review the applicable ICAR/NTA information bulletin for scholarship conditions.

sections.heading: Next academic and professional options
paragraphs: A common academic progression is a Ph.D. in an agriculture or allied-science specialization. ICAR’s common academic regulations present master’s and doctoral programmes within the same postgraduate framework, with a separate doctoral residential requirement of at least three academic years; the admitting institution’s current Ph.D. rules determine subject eligibility and selection.

For agricultural-research career tracks, the Agricultural Scientists Recruitment Board has published discipline-wise eligibility qualifications for its NET/ARS-related examinations. Its official combined notification shows that acceptable master’s qualifications are specialization-specific—for example, a master’s degree in Agriculture may be eligible only where paired with the required specialization for the relevant discipline. Candidates should therefore select and document their M.Sc. specialization carefully, and verify the current ASRB notification when an examination is announced.
bullets: Ph.D. study in the chosen agricultural specialization.
Research-oriented competitive examinations and posts where the current notification accepts the candidate’s exact master’s discipline/specialization.

Specialist roles in agricultural research, extension, crop/soil advisory, seed and plant-health fields, agribusiness, and higher education depend on the specialization, experience, employer criteria, and applicable recruitment rules.
